title: Scorecard rows 03/07 drop their chip + count to an orphan second line
problem: Rows with long principle names ('The universal destination of goods', 'Integral human development') overflow the body track, so .sc-body's flex-wrap:wrap (line 264) dumps the verdict chip and finding count onto a stranded, right-aligned second line while the other five rows keep them inline. Two of seven rows are twice the height of the others for no semantic reason, breaking the table rhythm and the row-to-row chip alignment the code's own comment (line 263) promises. Confirmed in before-scorecard-top-dark.png (row 03) in both themes and before-scorecard-mid-dark.png (row 07).
recommendation: Forbid the row from wrapping on desktop and let the principle NAME wrap internally instead — it already has min-width:0 and text-wrap:balance (line 138), so a long name becomes two balanced small-caps lines while §-range, chip and count stay on the first baseline. Trim the over-generous count column, and re-enable wrap only inside the existing 680px mobile query.
evidence: before-scorecard-top-dark.png / before-scorecard-top-light.png (row 03 wrapped, rows 01–02 inline), before-scorecard-mid-dark.png (row 07); final-rail-stars-v3.html lines 263–269, 138
amendments: Land together with C36 so the mobile scorecard rules are written once, not twice.
reasons: taste: Y — Two of seven rows breaking the table rhythm is visibly wrong in the report's primary navigation, and the fix restores rather than adds; coordinate the 680px wrap re-enable with C36's flex rewrite of .sc-row.

risk: Y — Pure CSS, scoped to .sc-* selectors; name wraps internally via existing min-width:0 + balance so nowrap cannot overflow the 588px body track, and mobile wrap is explicitly restored in the existing 680px block.

value: Y — Verified in before-scorecard-top-dark.png: rows 03/07 are double-height with chip+count stranded on a second line while siblings stay inline — the primary nav table looks broken on first scroll.

title: Mobile scorecard strands '01 ●' on its own line above the principle name
problem: At ≤680px .sc-row{grid-template-columns:1fr; row-gap:.4rem} (line 417) puts the ordinal+dot marker on its own row above the name (measured: marker row y=1814.8 h=17.3, name at y=1838.5). Each principle opens with a tiny orphaned number-and-dot line that reads as debris rather than the row's ordinal.
recommendation: Collapse .sc-row to a wrapped flex row on mobile so the marker sits inline before the name, letting the chip/count wrap naturally below.
evidence: /tmp/mh-mobile/mob-scorecard.png ('03 ●' alone above THE UNIVERSAL DESTINATION OF GOODS); final-rail-stars-v3.html lines 417, 421–423
reasons: taste: Y — '03 ●' stranded as debris above each principle name is visibly wrong on mobile; the flex collapse is minimal — coordinate with C01's desktop nowrap and 680px wrap rules so .sc-row is rewritten once.
risk: Y — The flex collapse verifies at 375px (16rem basis fits beside the marker) and is confined to the 680px block, leaving desktop grid untouched.
value: Y — '03 ●' stranded alone above each principle name reads as debris at the top of every mobile scorecard row.

title: Ratings key wraps mid-definition with a widowed 'it.' and a stranded '·' at line start
problem: The legend that teaches the whole rating system breaks as '…In tension — strains / it. · Violates — contradicts it.' — a two-word definition split across lines and a separator dot opening line 2 (confirmed in before-scorecard-top-dark.png and before-synthesis-dark.png). Root cause: ratingsKey() (lines 714–724) emits terms, definitions and '·' separators as one flat text run with no whitespace around the separator spans (their .5rem padding is not a break opportunity), and .ratings-key carries a one-off max-width:60ch (line 224) that guarantees the wrap lands mid-phrase.
recommendation: Wrap each 'Word — definition.' triple in a white-space:nowrap .rk-item span and put real spaces around each '·' so lines can only break at separators (longest item is ~24ch). Drop the 60ch override so the key inherits the shared measure (see C05), and reduce the separator padding to compensate for the real spaces.
evidence: before-scorecard-top-dark.png (lines 1–2 of the legend), before-scorecard-top-light.png, before-synthesis-dark.png (bottom); final-rail-stars-v3.html lines 223–228, 714–724
reasons: taste: Y — A legend that teaches the rating system must not widow its own two-word definition; nowrap items plus real break opportunities is exactly the invisible polish this page is about (coordinated, not conflicting, with C05's removal of the 60ch override).
risk: Y — Longest nowrap item (~24ch) fits any plausible measure, JS stays textContent-only, and dropping the 60ch override safely defers to the content-col cap; coordinates cleanly with C05 and C13.

value: Y — Verified: the legend that teaches the entire rating system reads 'strains / it. · Violates' — the first explanatory text of the scorecard looks typeset by accident; coordinate with C05/C13 which also touch .ratings-key.

title: Move the ratings key below 'The findings, by principle' caption
problem: renderScorecard() appends ratingsKey() BEFORE the h2 caption (lines 763–765), so the section opens with an unanchored legend sentence floating between the synthesis-closing rule and the heading it belongs to (before-scorecard-top-dark.png). Structurally, content precedes the very heading the section is aria-labelledby'd to (line 477) — poor document outline. A legend belongs under its section title, directly above the chips it explains.
recommendation: Swap the append order — caption first, then key — and retune margins so the key reads as the section's legend (slightly smaller, tighter to the first row) rather than stray body prose. Combine with C02's nowrap items.
evidence: before-scorecard-top-dark.png, before-synthesis-dark.png (legend above the caption); final-rail-stars-v3.html lines 477, 759–766
reasons: taste: Y — A legend floating above the heading it belongs to reads as a mistake and inverts the document outline; caption-then-key is the obvious quiet order (coordinate margins with C02).
risk: Y — A two-line append-order swap that also fixes the aria-labelledby outline (content currently precedes its own labelling h2); margins coordinate with C02 without overlap.

value: Y — Verified: the legend floats above the heading it belongs to, reading as orphaned synthesis text — moving it under the caption fixes comprehension and document outline together; sequence with C02.

title: Scorecard finding rows — the report's primary navigation — have no resting link affordance and snap on hover
problem: The global reset strips underlines (line 99) and the rows render in plain body ink; nothing at rest marks them as links except the small accent ✴, while the masthead and footer links are accent + underline (lines 214, 400) — two link grammars on one page, with the primary nav the unmarked one. On hover the only feedback is an instant color snap (lines 276–278) with no transition.
recommendation: Keep the quiet list aesthetic but give the title a hairline resting underline using the existing --ui-3 token, resolving to accent on hover/focus with a .15s ease — the same underline grammar as the masthead/footer links, dialed down.
css_js_sketch:
.scf-title{text-decoration:underline; text-decoration-thickness:1px; text-decoration-color:var(--ui-3); text-underline-offset:3px; transition:color .15s ease, text-decoration-color .15s ease}
.sc-findings a:hover .scf-title,.sc-findings a:focus-visible .scf-title{color:var(--accent); text-decoration-color:currentColor}
evidence: before-scorecard-top-dark.png / before-scorecard-top-light.png (rows read as static list items); final-rail-stars-v3.html lines 99, 214, 275–280, 400
reasons: taste: Y — Two link grammars on one page with the primary navigation unmarked is incoherent; a hairline --ui-3 resting underline resolving to accent is the existing grammar dialed down, not new decoration.
risk: Y — Decorative underline uses the existing --ui-3 token (no text-contrast requirement), and the transition is already neutralized by the reduced-motion block at line 410.
value: Y — The report's primary navigation is the one set of links with no resting affordance — readers who don't discover the rows are clickable lose the whole jump-to-finding workflow.

title: Pin the scorecard relation marker to the right rail — its column alignment is an accident of this dataset
problem: Only .scf-conf carries margin-left:auto (line 284); .scf-rel (line 282) sits flush after the title. The clean right-hand IN TENSION/VIOLATES + confidence column in the screenshots exists only because every title in this dataset happens to wrap to two lines at 1100px, forcing the title box to full width. Any one-line title (shorter finding, wider viewport, different audit) strands its relation label mid-line and shatters the column. A flagship template cannot depend on titles being long.
recommendation: Move the auto margin from the confidence number to the relation marker so the rel+conf pair is always pushed to the right edge regardless of title length; keep the existing fixed gap between them.
evidence: /tmp/sc-crops/relconf-col-dark.png (column aligns, but every title is 2 lines); final-rail-stars-v3.html lines 275, 282–284
reasons: taste: Y — A flagship template cannot let its right-hand relation column depend on every title happening to wrap; moving margin-left:auto to the rel marker makes the design intentional instead of accidental.
risk: Y — One-property flex change that removes a dataset-dependent alignment accident; title min-width:0 already guarantees shrink behavior with one-line titles.
value: Y — This is a template product, and the right-column alignment surviving only because every current title happens to wrap two lines is a latent break for the very next audit.

title: Scorecard's closing rule collides with its own full-measure hairlines
problem: The scorecard's internal group hairlines span the full grid (measured 162→937px) — correct, since the rail holds row numbers and chips right-align to that edge. But the rule that closes the section is nested in a .content-col (lines 852–854), so it runs 350→823px: starting 188px right of the hairlines above it and stopping 114px short of their right edge. At the scorecard/findings boundary the short rule reads as a glitch where the table system and the prose system collide.
recommendation: Let the one rule that closes the scorecard span the same full measure as the table's own hairlines — append it directly to the section instead of nesting it in the rail grid. Prose sections keep their content-edge rules; the table announces its own end on its own grid.
css_js_sketch:
/* renderScorecard, replacing lines 852–854 */
sec.appendChild(el("hr","rule block-rule"));  // spans rail+gutter+content like .sc-group borders
evidence: before-scorecard-mid-dark.png (hairlines x=162–937 vs closing rule x=350–823, y≈1064); final-rail-stars-v3.html lines 250, 852–854
reasons: taste: Y — The short rule colliding with the table's own full-grid hairlines reads as a glitch; letting the table close on its own grid is the coherent resolution of the two systems.
risk: Y — Appending the hr to the section instead of the rail grid is a two-line JS change; print's .grid display:block doesn't touch a direct section child, and mobile renders it identically.
value: Y — A closing rule starting 188px right of the hairlines directly above it reads as a rendering glitch at the most-trafficked section boundary.

title: Polish the hero verdict pill: baseline-align the OVERALL eyebrow, let it recede, and bind the pill to its caption
problem: Three small flaws compound on the page's single most important verdict. (1) .verdict-image uses align-items:center (line 165), so the .66rem mono eyebrow's baseline floats ~2px above the 1.02rem small-caps value — visible at reading size. (2) The eyebrow is explicitly opacity:1 (line 174) in the same color as the value with only a .55rem gap, so the pill mushes into one phrase, 'OVERALL TOWARD BABEL', label competing with verdict. (3) Spacing above the pill (1rem, line 233) and below it (1.2rem, line 238) are near-equal, so it floats ambiguously between the 'Synthesis' caption and the prose instead of reading as the section's headline. Keep the outline-only treatment — the 'distinct tier' styling is deliberate (comment, lines 162–163).
recommendation: Switch the pill to baseline alignment, let the eyebrow recede via opacity alone (no new borders or fills), and retune the caption/pill/prose margins so caption + verdict read as one head unit followed by the argument.
evidence: before-synthesis-dark.png (3x zoom: OVERALL baseline high; near-equal gaps above/below pill); final-rail-stars-v3.html lines 164–175, 231–238
amendments: Replace .vi-eyebrow{opacity:.72} with color:var(--tx-2) (or keep opacity:1): the composited .72 eyebrow measures 3.55:1 on dark violates — an AA failure at .66rem; baseline alignment and the margin retune are safe as sketched.
reasons: taste: Y — Baseline alignment, a receding eyebrow, and asymmetric spacing around the page's single most important verdict are exactly the polish a careful reader feels but never notices.
risk: Y — Two of three fixes are safe pure CSS, but the opacity recede provably breaks the dark-theme AA floor and needs the token-based variant.

value: Y — The eyebrow mushing into 'OVERALL TOWARD BABEL' mildly hurts the page's key verdict, but the baseline and margin tweaks are designer-grade; cheap enough to ride along — coordinate margins with C47.

title: Apply dedash() to scorecard titles so the same title reads identically in both places
problem: The finding card runs titles through dedash() so em-dashes become spaced en-dashes (line 935, the settled mono-title style), but the scorecard cross-reference uses curl() only (line 825) — the same sentence reads '…engagement — including dwell —…' in the scorecard and '…engagement – including dwell –…' on the card. A reader navigating scorecard→card sees the punctuation silently change.
recommendation: Apply dedash() in the scorecard too, making the title one canonical string everywhere (the en-dash also wraps better in the narrow bullet column).
reasons: taste: Y — The same title silently changing punctuation between scorecard and card breaks the reader's trust in precision; one canonical dedash'd string is a one-line fix.
risk: Y — One-line reuse of the existing dedash() helper at a single call site; makes titles canonical across both surfaces.
value: Y — Punctuation silently changing between the scorecard and the card it links to is a subtle consistency leak; one-word fix.

title: Empty-principle state: accessible count and a non-list note
problem: When a principle has zero engaging findings the count cell renders a bare '—' (line 810) — screen readers announce 'em dash' where siblings say 'N findings' — and the note is wrapped as ul > li > p (lines 839–847), announcing 'list, 1 item' for a single sentence. Not visible in this dataset (all seven principles engage), but the template ships the state and an honors-heavy audit will surface it.
recommendation: Keep the em dash visually but pair it with visually-hidden 'no findings' text; render the note as a grid-aligned div + p instead of a one-item list.
amendments: Also update the two mobile selectors at lines 418–420 (ul.sc-findings, ul.sc-findings > li) when loosening to .sc-findings, or the empty-state div loses its single-column collapse.
reasons: taste: Y — The template ships the empty-principle state even if this dataset doesn't exercise it; a screen reader announcing 'em dash' and a one-item list for a sentence are real flaws an honors-heavy audit will surface.
risk: Y — Sound a11y fix for a shipped-but-latent state; the selector loosening just needs to cover the 680px block too.
value: Y — Not reachable in this dataset, but the template ships the state and the next honors-heavy audit will surface the bare 'em dash' announcement.

title: Hide the empty-rail ghost rows at the 680px collapse
problem: Eight sections emit an empty el("div","rail") as the grid's first child (JS lines 664, 732, 750, 761, 852, 892, 1013, 1037). At ≤680px, .grid{grid-template-columns:1fr; row-gap:.5rem} (line 416) turns each into a 0-height first row plus a .5rem gap — a uniform 8px dead offset above every section, closing rule and ref-less citation cell (line 976).
recommendation: Hide empty marginalia cells when the grid collapses; the DOM-built divs are truly empty so :empty matches.
evidence: Measured at 375px: mast grid top 101.8, rail h=0, content-col top 109.8; final-rail-stars-v3.html lines 416, 664, 732, 750, 761, 852, 892, 976, 1013, 1037
reasons: taste: Y — A uniform 8px dead offset above every mobile section is exactly the kind of invisible slack a finished product doesn't carry; :empty on the DOM-built rails is the minimal fix.
risk: Y — :empty provably matches — el() creates the rail divs with no child nodes at all — and the rule is scoped inside the existing mobile query.
value: Y — A uniform 8px dead offset on mobile reads as spacing, not breakage, but the :empty rule is a free two-line cleanup.
